exemplar.title: Should The Body Shop refresh their brand identity in order to adapt to market saturation in the cruelty-free cosmetics industry?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should The Body Shop refresh their brand identity in order to adapt to market saturation in the cruelty-free cosmetics industry?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Correctly identifies and applies the key concept of change to major events (acquisitions, brand refresh, market saturation)
Consistently links discussion points back to The Body Shopâs organizational context
weaknesses: Use of explicit conceptual language is intermittent and often descriptive rather than fully analytical
Early sections lack a clear framing that weaves the concept of change into the narrative from the outset
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student demonstrates a clear understanding of the key concept of change and ties it to The Body Shopâs situation, integration remains uneven. References to change are sometimes descriptive and not fully woven through the analysis, matching the 3-mark band rather than the higher band where conceptual language is seamlessly integrated throughout.

strengths: Uses exactly five supporting documents, meeting the quantitative requirement
Most documents are relevant and contribute useful context and examples to the analysis
weaknesses: Depth of analysis variesâtwo sources are brief news reports and one is dated (2011), limiting full relevance
Bibliography is incomplete, with only two entries despite five documents being referenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides the correct number of supporting documents and selects generally relevant material. However, uneven depth and occasional dated sources prevent full immersion in the top band. This aligns with the ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ 3-mark level.

strengths: Appropriate choice of mainstream tools (SWOT and STEEPLE) that are clearly defined
Tools are applied to The Body Shop and linked to elements of the research question
weaknesses: Application remains largely descriptive with limited critical or analytical insight
Relevance of some tool components to the research question is not always explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools is adequate but their application is more descriptive than analytical, fitting the 2-mark level rather than higher bands that demand clear, effective and critical application to the research question.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Includes some analytical comments linking SWOT/STEEPLE factors to the research question
Uses both quantitative (Mintel statistic) and qualitative (poll review) data sources
weaknesses: Data selection is superficial and limited in scope, with little recent or comparative quantitative evidence
Analysis is patchy and narrative rather than deeply evaluative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The superficial selection and use of data combined with moderately shallow analysis corresponds to the 2-mark band. While there are some connections to the research question, depth and integration are insufficient for higher marks.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, identifying key causes and recommending a brand refresh
Ties conclusions back to main findings in the body of the IA
weaknesses: Conclusions rely on limited evidence and do not cite specific data points
Lacks stronger reference to the central concept of change in the concluding remarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion answers the research question but is only partially supported by evidence and does not fully integrate data or conceptual links. This places it in the 2-mark band for conclusions.

strengths: Clear table of contents and consistent use of headings
Logical progression of sections reflects appropriate IA structure
weaknesses: Minor lapses (e.g., an incomplete SWOT section) indicate occasional structural gaps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is appropriate and guides the reader effectively. A small incomplete section prevents the award of higher marks, aligning with the 2-mark band.

strengths: All required elements are present (title page, word count, methodology, bibliography, documents)
Presentation is clear and follows IB guidelines for including necessary components
weaknesses: Bibliography formatting is inconsistent and could follow a formal style more closely
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA includes every required presentation element, meeting the full 2-mark band for presentation despite minor formatting inconsistencies.

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, headings and logical progression demonstrate appropriate structure throughout, meeting the descriptor for 2 marks.

All documents are relevant to the research question, but depth varies: two are brief news reports and one dates from 2011, limiting currency. The evidence provides useful ideas but not a wide range of data or perspectives, so the work sits in the ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ category rather than the top band.
The IA includes the title page, word count, methodology, bibliography and supporting documents, satisfying all required presentation elements, hence the full 2 marks.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed and used, meeting the rubric requirement for the highest band under ânumber of documentsâ.

Both tools are applied to The Body Shop and linked to elements of the research question, but the discussion often remains descriptive (e.g., listing STEEPLE factors). Relevance is generally clear, yet application is not consistently analytical, matching the âadequately appliedâ 3-mark level.

Data use is limited to a Mintel statistic and qualitative information from the news articles; little quantitative or comparative data is deployed. Selection is therefore superficial, corresponding to the 2-mark band.

The SWOT and STEEPLE sections contain some analytical comment linking factors to the research question, but integration is patchy and depth moderate. This is stronger than âlimited analysisâ yet not âmostly effective with integrationâ, so 3 marks are appropriate.

The student explicitly answers the research question, identifying market saturation and brand dilution as principal causes and recommending a brand refresh. This fulfils the top band requirement for answering the RQ.
The student chooses two mainstream strategic tools (SWOT and STEEPLE). This represents an adequate, though not extensive, selection and clearly fits the 3-mark descriptor for selection.
The conclusion aligns broadly with earlier arguments but rests on limited evidence and does not reference data directly, showing only partial consistency. This aligns with the 2-mark description.

Throughout the IA the student links points directly to The Body Shopâs situation (administration, takeover by LâOrÃ©al, comparison with Lush, potential AurÃ©a rescue). These links move beyond description to analysis, yet occasionally lapse into general commentary. The organisation is nevertheless central to every section, showing strong but not fully seamless integration, which best matches the 4-mark band.

The student correctly selects the key concept of change and refers to it when discussing acquisitions, brand-identity refresh and market saturation. However, explicit conceptual language and integration are intermittent; most references to change are descriptive rather than woven through the analysis. This reflects a clear, but not yet fully integrated, use of the concept, aligning with the 3-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: The title page clearly presents the research question, key concept, and word count, fulfilling all required elements and aiding reader orientation.
While the key concept of change is stated, the introduction would benefit from explicitly framing early arguments in conceptual terms to weave change into the narrative.
Including the word count is good practice, but ensure consistency by placing it in the footer area per IB presentation guidelines.
The table of contents is well organized, with clear headings that mirror the IA structure and guide the reader effectively.
The opening background paragraph gives context but could better link to the concept of change by discussing how The Body Shopâs identity evolved over time.
The Mintel statistic is relevant but dated; incorporating more recent consumer data would strengthen the analysis and show effective data selection.
Methodology precisely explains why each supporting document was chosen and how it will inform the analysis, exhibiting good alignment with research aims.
The rationale for selecting SWOT and STEEPLE is appropriate but the discussion remains descriptive; deepen the analysis by explicitly linking each factor to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The description of internal strengths captures the brand legacy but lacks quantitative evidence; including sales or loyalty metrics would reinforce the claim.
The weakness discussion uses supporting document 3 effectively but could integrate the concept of market saturation more explicitly to deepen analytical insight.
The SWOT strengths section ends mid-sentence under opportunities, indicating an incomplete thought. Ensure all analysis points are fully articulated.
Threats are described comprehensively, but the analysis remains largely narrative. Introduce comparative market data or trend analysis to elevate depth.
The STEEPLE introduction succinctly defines the tool and its factors, demonstrating clarity in tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The social factor analysis rightly highlights market saturation but would benefit from linking these societal changes to specific revenue or market share trends.
Economic factor discussion connects financial struggles to wider economic pressures, but referencing GDP or industry growth data would strengthen the point.
The conclusion articulates key findings and answers the research question explicitly, reflecting consistency with evidence presented earlier.
The conclusion would be more persuasive if it cited key data points (e.g., percentage closures or revenue decline) and tied them back to the concept of change.
Only two bibliography entries are listed despite five supporting documents being used; ensure all sources are included in the bibliography.
Bibliography entries lack consistent formatting; adopting a formal referencing style (e.g., Harvard) will improve presentation and academic rigor.
Supporting document 1 effectively provides context on store closures and redundancies, offering concrete figures for analysis.
Student recounts redundancy numbers but misses the opportunity to calculate closure percentages relative to total stores; include this for deeper evaluation.
Supporting document 2 is recent and highly relevant, enhancing the depth of secondary evidence.
The write-up of document 2 summaries facts without critique; consider applying transaction cost theory to explain why Aureliusâs acquisition strategy failed.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Supporting document 3 provides unique qualitative insights, but its age (2011) limits relevance to current market dynamics.
The poll review adds a primary perspective on consumer experience, enriching the qualitative analysis.
Supporting document 4 insightfully outlines the AurÃ©a deal, showing how change may revive the brand; this connects well to the key concept.
The discussion of AurÃ©aâs acquisition could be enriched by comparing its sustainability track record against The Body Shopâs original values.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Supporting document 5âs influencer strategy analysis complements the technological STEEPLE factor, illustrating relevant digital trends.
While the influencer strategy is described well, assess past green campaignsâ effectiveness with engagement or ROI metrics to deepen evaluation.
